channel.title: Worklog on Internship Report
channel.description: Recent content in Worklog on Internship Report

item.title: Worklog Week 1
item.description:
Week 1 Goals:

  • Master foundational knowledge about AWS Cloud Platform, basic services, and cloud architecture
  • Get familiar with AWS management tools: AWS Management Console & AWS CLI
  • Master concepts about cost management, IAM security, and VPC networking
  • Practice Infrastructure as Code to automate deployment on AWS
  • Build good relationships with members in First Cloud AI Journey

Tasks to complete this week:

Day Task Start Date Completion Date Reference Material
2 Get acquainted with members and mentors at FCAJ
Read and note regulations and policies at the internship unit
Study 5 theory videos from FCAJ Workforce Bootcamp - 2026:
  + Introduction to AWS
  + What is FCAJ and the knowledge you will receive
  + Generative AI on AWS: Kuro AI
  + Management Console Guide: Sign in ROOT USER & IAM USER
  + Cost optimization on AWS
  + AWS Global Infrastructure: Data center, Availability Zone, Region, Cost optimization, Edge location		 05/04/2026 05/04/2026 https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Gz56QzLQ_Yo&list=PLahN4TLWtox3TSYFbN1DNX7NZgTVXNj3x
3 Learn basics of drawing AWS architecture on draw.io
Explore AWS and service types
  + Basic infrastructure services
  + Storage and Databases
  + Management and Optimization
05/05/2026 05/05/2026 https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=l8isyDe-GwY&list=PLahN4TLWtox2a3vElknwzU_urND8hLn1i&index=2
4 Hands-on lab: Managing AWS spending with AWS Budgets
  + Create Cost Budget
  + Create Budget
  + Create Usage Budget
  + Create Reserved Instance Budget
  + Create Savings Plans Budget
- Learn about AWS support requests		 05/06/2026 05/06/2026 https://000007.awsstudygroup.com/vi/
https://000009.awsstudygroup.com/vi/3-manage-cases/
5 Hands-on lab: Managing access with AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM)
  + Introduction to IAM
  + Create IAM group and IAM user
  + Create IAM role and operation user
  + Switch IAM Role
Hands-on lab: Amazon Virtual Private Cloud (VPC)
  + Introduction to Amazon VPC: Subnets, Route Table, Internet Gateway, NAT Gateway
  + Firewall in VPC
  + Preparation steps before deploying to EC2
  + Deploy EC2 instance		 05/07/2026 05/08/2026 https://000003.awsstudygroup.com/vi/1-introduce/
https://000002.awsstudygroup.com/vi/
6 Hands-on lab continued: Amazon Virtual Private Cloud (VPC)
  + Introduction to site-to-site VPN
  + Hands-on practice:
   • Create a VPN environment
   • Configure VPN connection
   • Configure VPN using strongSwan with Transit Gateway
  + Automate VPC deployment with Infrastructure as Code
Review Week 1 knowledge		 05/08/2026 05/08/2026 https://000003.awsstudygroup.com/vi/1-introduce/

Week 1 Results Achieved:

  • Understand basic AWS services and service groups:
    + Compute (EC2)
    + Networking (VPC, Route 53)
    + Cost Management (AWS Budgets, Cost Explorer)
    + Security (IAM, Security Groups)

item.title: Worklog Week 2
item.description:
Week 2 Objectives:

  • Build a solid foundation in Amazon EC2 and its core features such as Instance types, AMI, Snapshots
  • Practice deploying a Node.js application on EC2 instances running Amazon Linux and Windows
  • Understand how to manage access permissions and limit resource usage using AWS IAM
  • Get familiar with the AWS Cloud9 IDE for developing applications directly on the cloud
  • Master hosting a static website on Amazon S3 with advanced features
  • Optimize website performance using CloudFront CDN
  • Manage object versioning and perform data replication between regions on S3

Tasks to complete this week:

Day Task Start Date Completion Date Reference Materials
2 Hands-on lab: Getting started and deploying an application on Amazon Compute Cloud (Amazon EC2)
  + Introduction to AWS (Amazon Elastic Compute Cloud) - EC2
   • Instance type
   • AMI/Backup/Keypair
  + Preparation steps:
   • Create VPC for Linux/Windows
   • Security group for Linux/Windows
  + Launch a Windows/Linux instance
  + EC2 basics:
   • Change EC2 configuration
   • Create a snapshot
   • Custom AMI
   • Create an instance from a Custom AMI
   • Access EC2 after losing the keypair (Linux and Windows)
   • Configure a desktop GUI for EC2-ubuntu 22.04
   • Amazon EBS Snapshot archive		 05/11/2026 05/12/2026 https://000004.awsstudygroup.com/vi/5-amazonec2basic/
3 Continue completing the EC2 hands-on lab
  + Deploy a Node.js app on Amazon Linux:
   • Download the LAMP web server
   • Prepare LAMP
   • Configure and install phpMyAdmin
   • Install Node.js on Linux
   • Deploy the app on the Linux instance
  + Deploy a Node.js app on Amazon Windows:
   • Install XAMPP on the Windows instance
   • Install Node.js on the Windows instance
   • Deploy on the Windows instance
  + Limit resource usage using the IAM service
   • Restrict usage to a specific Region
   • Restrict EC2 by instance family
   • Restrict EC2 by instance type
   • Restrict EBS volume
   • Restrict resource deletion permissions
   • Restrict resource deletion by time window
Hands-on lab: Granting application access to AWS services with IAM Role
  + Create an EC2 instance
  + Create an S3 bucket
  + Use an access key: create an IAM user, access key, and use the access key
  + Create an IAM role
  + Use the IAM role		 05/12/2026 05/12/2026 https://000048.awsstudygroup.com/vi/3-iamroleec2/
https://000004.awsstudygroup.com/vi/5-amazonec2basic/
4 Hands-on lab: Using a browser-based Cloud IDE with AWS Cloud9
  + Overview of AWS Cloud9
  + Create a Cloud9 instance
  + Basic features:
   • Using the command line
   • Working with text files
   • Dashboard interface
  + Using the AWS CLI
Hands-on lab: Hosting a static website with Amazon S3
  + General theoretical overview
  + Create an S3 bucket
  + Upload data
  + Enable static website hosting
  + Configure Block Public Access
  + Configure public object access
  + Test the website		 05/13/2026 05/14/2026 https://000057.awsstudygroup.com/vi/1-introduce/
https://000049.awsstudygroup.com/vi/
5 Hands-on lab: Hosting a static website with Amazon S3 (continued)
  + Speed up the static website with CloudFront
  + Block public access to S3
  + Configure Amazon CloudFront
  + Verify Amazon CloudFront
  + Bucket versioning:
   • Introduction
   • How it works
   • Step-by-step guide
  + Move an Object
  + Replicate an S3 Object to another region		 05/14/2026 05/14/2026 https://000057.awsstudygroup.com/vi/1-introduce/

Week 2 Results Achieved

EC2 Core Knowledge:

item.title: Worklog Week 3
item.description:
Week 3 Objectives:

  • Gain a solid foundational understanding of Amazon Relational Database Service (Amazon RDS) and its database management features
  • Practice creating and configuring an RDS database instance on AWS
  • Understand how to set up VPC, Security Groups, and DB Subnet Groups for RDS
  • Practice Backup and Restore operations on RDS
  • Get familiar with Amazon Lightsail for deploying lightweight, cost-effective applications
  • Understand how to deploy applications on Lightsail (WordPress, PrestaShop, Akaunting)
  • Grasp the concepts of Lightsail management features such as Snapshots, instance scaling, and monitoring

Tasks to be carried out this week:

Day Task Start Date Completion Date Reference Source
2 Hands-on lab: Creating a database on Amazon Relational Database Service (Amazon RDS)
  + Overview and introduction
  + Preparation steps:
   • Create a VPC
   • Create an EC2 security group
   • Create an RDS subnet group
   • Create a DB Subnet group
  + Create an EC2 instance
  + Create an RDS database instance
  + Deploy the application
  + Backup and restore		 05/18/2026 05/18/2026 https://000005.awsstudygroup.com/vi/
3 Hands-on lab: Optimizing computing costs with Amazon Lightsail
  + Overview and introduction
  + Deploy a Lightsail database
  + Deploy a WordPress Instance:
   • Deploy the instance
   • Configure Ubuntu
   • Configure Networking
   • Configure WordPress
  + Deploy a PrestaShop E-Commerce Instance:
   • Deploy the instance
   • Configure Networking
   • Deploy the application
  + Deploy an Akaunting Instance:
   • Deploy the instance
   • Configure networking
   • Deploy the application
  + Secure the application
  + Create a Snapshot
  + Switch to a larger instance as application resource usage increases
  + Set up alerts for high CPU resource usage		 05/19/2026 05/20/2026 https://000045.awsstudygroup.com/vi/8-create-alarms/
4 Hands-on lab: Automatically scaling an application with Amazon EC2 Autoscaling
  + Overview and benefits of an Auto Scaling Group
  + Introduction to Auto Scaling Group
  + Preparation steps:
   • Set up the network infrastructure
   • Initialize the EC2 instance
   • Initialize a Database instance with RDS
   • Install the database data
   • Deploy the web server
   • Prepare metrics for Predictive Scaling
   • Create a Launch Template		 05/20/2026 05/20/2026 https://000006.awsstudygroup.com/vi/1-introduction/
5 Continued hands-on lab: Automatically scaling an application with Amazon EC2 Autoscaling
  + Set up the load balancer
  + Check the results
  + Create an auto scaling group
  + Test the solutions:
   • Manual scaling
   • Scheduled scaling
   • Dynamic scaling
   • Predictive scaling
  + Review the Autoscaling lab		 05/21/2026 05/21/2026 https://000006.awsstudygroup.com/vi/1-introduction/

Week 3 Results Achieved

RDS Core Knowledge:

item.title: Worklog Week 4
item.description:
Week 4 Goals:

  • Master knowledge about Amazon CloudWatch for monitoring and tracking AWS resources
  • Practice creating metrics, logs, alarms, and dashboards on CloudWatch
  • Understand how to set up hybrid DNS with Route 53 Resolver
  • Get familiar with AWS CLI and how to use it on EC2 instances
  • Practice AWS CLI with various AWS services (S3, SNS, IAM, VPC)
  • Review knowledge of basic services: EC2, VPC, IAM Role, S3

Tasks to complete this week:

Day Task Start Date Completion Date Reference Material
2 Hands-on lab: Creating a system monitoring dashboard with Amazon CloudWatch
  + Introduction to CloudWatch
  + Setting up AWS CloudFormation Stack
  + CloudWatch Metrics:
   • View metrics
   • Perform searches
   • Perform mathematical operations
   • Create dynamic labels
  + CloudWatch Logs:
   • CloudWatch Logs Insight
   • CloudWatch Metric Filters
  + CloudWatch Alarms
  + CloudWatch Dashboards		 05/25/2026 05/25/2026 https://000008.awsstudygroup.com/vi/
3 Hands-on lab: Setting up Hybrid DNS with Route 53 Resolver
  + Introduction to Route 53
  + Preparation steps:
   • Create keypair
   • Create Cloud Formation Template
   • Initialize security group
   • Connect to RDGW (Remote Desktop Gateway)
   • Deploy Microsoft AD
   • Setup DNS
  + Create Route 53 Outbound Endpoint
  + Create Route 53 Resolver Rules
  + Create Route 53 Inbound Endpoints
  + Test result		 05/26/2026 05/26/2026 https://000010.awsstudygroup.com/vi/
4 Hands-on lab: Using AWS CLI on Amazon EC2 (Windows/Ubuntu)
  + Familiarize with AWS CLI:
   • What is AWS CLI?
   • Supported environments
   • AWS CLI capabilities
   • Output formats
   • Basic configuration
  + IAM User Group:
   • IAM User
   • AWS Access Key
   • EC2 Instance and SSH access permission setup
   • Install AWS CLI
  + Check resources via CLI
  + AWS CLI with Amazon S3
  + AWS CLI with Amazon SNS
  + AWS CLI with Amazon IAM
  + AWS CLI with Amazon VPC
  + Create EC2 and use AWS CLI
  + Troubleshooting		 05/27/2026 05/27/2026 https://000011.awsstudygroup.com/vi/1-introduce/
5 Review services from previous labs
  + EC2
  + VPC
  + IAM Role (AWS IAM)
  + S3 bucket		 05/28/2026 05/28/2026 https://000004.awsstudygroup.com/vi/
https://000003.awsstudygroup.com/vi/
https://000048.awsstudygroup.com/vi/
https://000057.awsstudygroup.com/vi/

Week 4 Results Achieved

Amazon CloudWatch Knowledge:

item.title: Worklog Week 5
item.description:
Week 5 Objectives:

  • Build a solid understanding of how to optimize EC2 costs using AWS Lambda
  • Practice creating Lambda functions to automate EC2 instance control
  • Review all knowledge learned from previous weeks (Week 1-4)
  • Systematize basic AWS services and their usage

Tasks to complete this week:

Day Task Start Date Completion Date Reference Materials
2 Hands-on lab: Optimize EC2 costs with Lambda
  + Introduction
  + Preparation steps:
   • Create a VPC
   • Create a Security Group
   • EC2 instance
   • Incoming Web-hooks stack
   • Create tags for the instance
   • Create a role for Lambda
  + Create a Lambda function:
   • Stop function
   • Start function
  + Verify results		 06/01/2026 06/01/2026 https://000022.awsstudygroup.com/vi/
3 Review content for each week
  + Week 1: AWS Foundation & IAM
  + Week 2: EC2, S3, CloudFront, CloudFormation
  + Week 3: RDS, Lightsail, EC2 Autoscaling
 Create Worklog + Week 1 and Week 2
 06/02/2026 06/02/2026 https://000004.awsstudygroup.com/vi/
https://000005.awsstudygroup.com/vi/
https://000045.awsstudygroup.com/vi/
4 Review content for each week
  + Week 4: CloudWatch, Route 53 Resolver, AWS CLI
  Create Worklog + Week 3 and Week 4		 06/03/2026 06/03/2026 https://000008.awsstudygroup.com/vi/
https://000010.awsstudygroup.com/vi/
https://000011.awsstudygroup.com/vi/
5 Create Worklog
+ Week 5
Plan for Workshop
+ Review team members’ ideas
+ Schedule meeting times
+ Research sample workshop projects and proposal writing
+ Understand workshop requirements and proposals
+ Understand the architecture based on the team’s ideas		 06/04/2026 06/04/2026

Week 5 Results Achieved

AWS Lambda Knowledge:

item.title: Worklog Week 6
item.description:
Week 6 Objectives:

  • Explore the Modernize lab group on AWS Cloud Journey.
  • Understand the concepts of application modernization, serverless architecture, and event-driven processing.
  • Connect lab knowledge with the team’s workshop ideas and proposal.
  • Participate in the AWS AI & Cloud Operations Workshop and note content applicable to the project.

Tasks to be carried out this week:

Day Task Start Date Completion Date Reference Source
2 Study the Modernize lab group
  + Read the overview of modernize application content
  + Understand how to transform applications toward cloud-native direction
  + Take notes on services that may be related to the workshop		 06/08/2026 06/08/2026 https://cloudjourney.awsstudygroup.com/vi/4-modernize/
3 Practice serverless and event-driven content
  + Review Lambda, SQS, S3 event notification
  + Understand how to separate asynchronous processing using queues
  + Connect with the image processing pipeline of the proposal		 06/09/2026 06/09/2026 https://cloudjourney.awsstudygroup.com/vi/4-modernize/
4 Research modernization in the direction of operations and monitoring
  + Explore logging, monitoring, CloudWatch
  + Take notes on system observation requirements for Lambda, SQS, S3, and DynamoDB		 06/10/2026 06/10/2026 https://cloudjourney.awsstudygroup.com/vi/4-modernize/
5 Prepare content related to workshop/proposal
  + Summarize AWS services that can be used in the architecture
  + Review the idea of automating product quality monitoring
  + Prepare questions and notes for the workshop session		 06/11/2026 06/11/2026 https://cloudjourney.awsstudygroup.com/vi/4-modernize/
6 Attend AWS AI & Cloud Operations Workshop
  + Record content on AgenticOps, AI Voice, and applications of AI in cloud operations
  + Connect workshop knowledge with the serverless image processing topic
  + Write a summary of the event		 06/12/2026 06/12/2026 AWS AI & Cloud Operations Workshop

Week 6 Results Achieved

Modernize Application Knowledge:

item.title: Worklog Week 7
item.description:
Week 7 Objectives:

  • Learn labs directly related to workshop and proposal content.
  • Hold a team meeting to discuss, divide tasks, and align implementation scope.
  • Write work logs, update progress, and document completed sections.
  • Complete the architectural direction for the serverless image processing system.

Tasks to be carried out this week:

Day Task Start Date Completion Date Reference Source
2 Learn labs related to the workshop
  + Review IAM Role, S3, SQS, and Lambda
  + Identify the role of each service in the image processing system
  + Take notes on sections to include in the workshop		 06/15/2026 06/15/2026 https://cloudjourney.awsstudygroup.com/vi/
3 Research proposal content
  + Review the product quality monitoring problem
  + Identify the problem, proposed solution, and expected results
  + Prepare the overall architecture description section		 06/16/2026 06/16/2026 Proposal
4 Hold team meeting to discuss task handover
  + Align workshop scope
  + Divide content preparation, images, and reports
  + Record tasks that need to be completed in the next week		 06/17/2026 06/17/2026
5 Write work logs
  + Update worklog with learned content
  + Record proposal and workshop progress
  + Summarize missing issues that need to be supplemented		 06/18/2026 06/18/2026

Week 7 Results Achieved

Workshop Preparation:

item.title: Worklog Week 8
item.description:
Week 8 Goals:

  • Draw the structure/architecture of the project within 2 days.
  • Finalize the architecture diagram for the proposal and workshop.
  • Practice the main content in the workshop.
  • Start editing workshop content into a clear report file.

Tasks to complete this week:

Day Task Start Date Completion Date Reference Material
2 Draw project structure - Day 1
  + Identify architecture layers: Frontend/Auth, Storage, Backend/AI-ML, Monitoring/Security
  + Sketch the processing flow from image upload to result storage
  + Select appropriate AWS services for each layer		 06/22/2026 06/22/2026 Proposal
3 Draw project structure - Day 2
  + Finalize architecture diagram
  + Review the S3 -> SQS -> Lambda -> Rekognition/Textract -> DynamoDB flow
  + Add CloudWatch and IAM to the diagram		 06/23/2026 06/23/2026 DiagramStructure
4 Practice workshop content
  + Prepare IAM Role for Lambda
  + Create SQS queue to receive image processing events
  + Configure S3 bucket and event notification		 06/24/2026 06/24/2026 Workshop
5 Edit workshop report
  + Review overview, IAM, SQS, S3 sections
  + Standardize descriptions for each practice step
  + Add illustrative images and verification results		 06/25/2026 06/25/2026 Workshop

Week 8 Results Achieved

Architecture Design:

item.title: Worklog Week 9
item.description:
Week 9 Objectives:

  • Continue practicing the remaining content in the workshop.
  • Complete the workshop report file from the hands-on steps.
  • Review the processing flow, illustrations, and content cleanup.
  • Verify consistency between proposal, diagram, and workshop.

Tasks to complete this week:

Day Task Start Date Completion Date Reference Materials
2 Hands-on Lambda function
  + Create a Lambda function to process messages from SQS
  + Configure necessary permissions
  + Check logs on CloudWatch		 06/29/2026 06/29/2026 Lambda Workshop
3 Testing and validation
  + Upload images to S3 to create events
  + Check messages passing through SQS
  + Monitor Lambda processing and logging		 06/30/2026 06/30/2026 Testing
4 Edit workshop report
  + Review writing style of each section
  + Add missing illustrations
  + Check image paths and titles		 07/01/2026 07/01/2026 Workshop
5 Complete cleanup and summary
  + Document the steps to delete resources to avoid additional costs
  + Review the entire workshop report
  + Compare content with proposal		 07/02/2026 07/02/2026 Cleanup

Week 9 Results Achieved

Workshop Completion:

item.title: Worklog Week 10
item.description:
Week 10 Objectives:

  • Learn the lab courses in the Data and Analytics group on AWS Cloud Journey.
  • Master basic knowledge about data collection, storage, processing, and analysis on AWS.
  • Connect data analytics knowledge with the need for reporting damaged product data in the proposal.
  • Supplement ideas for improving dashboards, statistics, and data queries for the project.

Tasks to be carried out this week:

Day Task Start Date Completion Date Reference Source
2 Learn Data and Analytics Overview
  + Read the introduction content of the lab group
  + Take notes on commonly used data services on AWS
  + Identify parts that can be applied to the project		 07/06/2026 07/06/2026 https://cloudjourney.awsstudygroup.com/vi/6-dataandanalytic/
3 Explore Data Storage and Processing
  + Review the role of S3 and DynamoDB
  + Understand how to store processing history and analysis results
  + Take notes on data query requirements over time		 07/07/2026 07/07/2026 https://cloudjourney.awsstudygroup.com/vi/6-dataandanalytic/
4 Explore Data Analysis and Reporting
  + Identify key metrics to track: number of images processed, error rate, processing status
  + Propose a reporting direction for warehouse management
  + Connect with DynamoDB and CloudWatch metrics		 07/08/2026 07/08/2026 https://cloudjourney.awsstudygroup.com/vi/6-dataandanalytic/
5 Divide the team to write 3 Blogs
  + Search for AWS blog sources
  + Research the structure of blog posts
  + Finalize Week 10 worklog		 07/09/2026 07/09/2026 https://cloudjourney.awsstudygroup.com/vi/6-dataandanalytic/

Week 10 Results Achieved

Data and Analytics Knowledge:

item.title: Worklog Week 11
item.description:
Week 11 Objectives:

  • Review the entire proposal, workshop, and worklog written.
  • Supplement missing content related to operations, security, and cost optimization.
  • Check consistency between architecture, practical content, and reports.
  • Prepare a polished version for presentation/evaluation.

Tasks to be carried out this week:

Day Task Start Date Completion Date Reference Source
2 Search for blog post information
  + Check the problem, solution, and architecture sections
  + Compare AWS services used and blog content. Proceed with writing blogs to post on Facebook		 07/13/2026 07/13/2026
3 Review the workshop
  + Check the order of practice steps
  + Check images and operation descriptions
  + Supplement the explanation of test results		 07/14/2026 07/14/2026 Workshop
4 Supplement operations and security content
  + Check IAM least privilege
  + Supplement notes on CloudWatch logs/metrics
  + Note cleanup to avoid incurring costs		 07/15/2026 07/15/2026 Cleanup
5 Finalize the worklog
  + Update worklogs for missing weeks
  + Standardize the format of task tables
  + Check internal links		 07/16/2026 07/16/2026

Week 11 Results Achieved

Review & Improvement:

item.title: Worklog Week 12
item.description:
Week 12 Objectives:

  • Summarize the learning, practice, and workshop development process.
  • Complete the final documentation parts.
  • Prepare content for presenting project results.
  • Extract lessons learned about AWS knowledge, teamwork, and technical report writing.

Tasks to complete this week:

Day Task Start Date Completion Date Reference Materials
2 Summarize AWS knowledge learned
  + Review EC2, S3, IAM, Lambda, SQS, DynamoDB, CloudWatch
  + Consolidate services applied to proposal/workshop
  + Document knowledge areas that need improvement		 07/20/2026 07/20/2026
3 Complete final documentation
  + Review proposal
  + Review workshop
  + Review worklog and events participated		 07/21/2026 07/21/2026 Proposal
4 Prepare project presentation
  + Summarize problem statement and solution
  + Prepare architecture explanation section
  + Prepare demo/workshop walkthrough section		 07/22/2026 07/22/2026 Workshop
5 Lessons learned and final summary
  + Document lessons on teamwork
  + Document lessons on serverless design on AWS
  + Complete week 12 worklog		 07/23/2026 07/23/2026

Week 12 Results Achieved

Final Summary:
